section.heading: PFAS destruction and disposal guidance

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 1 year after December 20, 2019, the Administrator shall publish interim guidance on the destruction and disposal of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances and materials containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, including—

paragraph.content: textiles, other than consumer goods, treated with perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances;
spent filters, membranes, resins, granular carbon, and other waste from water treatment;
landfill leachate containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances; and
solid, liquid, or gas waste streams containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances from facilities manufacturing or using perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.

subsection.heading: Considerations; inclusions
subsection.chapeau: The interim guidance under subsection (a) shall—

subparagraph.content: the potential for releases of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances during destruction or disposal, including through volatilization, air dispersion, or leachate; and
potentially vulnerable populations living near likely destruction or disposal sites; and

paragraph.content: provide guidance on testing and monitoring air, effluent, and soil near potential destruction or disposal sites for releases described in paragraph (1)(A).

content.p: The Administrator shall publish revisions to the interim guidance under subsection (a) as the Administrator determines to be appropriate, but not less frequently than once every 3 years.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 116–92, div. F, title LXXIII, § 7361, Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 2289.)
